title: Create a Song
description: Use Song Mode to arrange multiple patterns into a complete song structure with intro, verse, chorus, and outro.

steps.title: Create Multiple Patterns
steps.instruction: Before using Song Mode, create at least 2-3 different patterns. Use pattern 1 for a verse, pattern 2 for a chorus, etc.

substeps.instruction: Create Pattern 1: Your verse or intro section
Create Pattern 2: Your chorus or main section
Optionally create Pattern 3: Bridge or breakdown

steps.tip: Patterns can be different lengths. A 4-bar intro, 8-bar verse, and 8-bar chorus is a good starting structure.

steps.title: Enter Song Mode
steps.instruction: Hold SHIFT and press TEMPO/LOOP to enter Song Mode. The display will show 'SONG' and the current song position.

steps.title: Set Position 1 (Intro)
steps.instruction: You're at song position 1. Use the number keys to select which pattern/scene plays here. For an intro, you might select pattern 1.
steps.tip: A scene combines all 4 groups. If Group A is on pattern 1 and Group B is on pattern 1, that's your scene.

steps.title: Move to Position 2
steps.instruction: Press + to move to song position 2. This is where you'll set your first verse or main section.

steps.title: Set Position 2 (Verse)
steps.instruction: Select the pattern for position 2. If you want the verse to repeat, you can set it to play multiple times before moving to the next position.

steps.title: Continue Building the Song
steps.instruction: Continue adding song positions for your complete arrangement. A typical song might have 8-12 positions.

steps.title: Play the Song
steps.instruction: Press PLAY while in Song Mode to hear your complete song arrangement. The EP-133 will progress through each position automatically.
steps.tip: You can still manually trigger pattern changes during playback for live performance variations.

outcomes: Understanding of Song Mode and song positions
Ability to arrange patterns into a complete song
Knowledge of typical song structure (intro, verse, chorus, outro)
